channel.title: NBC Sports Fantasy Football News
channel.description: Latest player news and analysis for fantasy football from NBC Sports.

item.title: CBS’ Jonathan Jones reports teams believe Osa Odighizuwa “can now be traded for.”
item.description: After restructuring Kenny Clark, the Cowboys appear to be leaning towards keeping him by guaranteeing him money this year. With Quinnen Williams also at defensive tackle, that room is pretty full. It wouldn’t be surprising if the Cowboys traded Odighizuwa, but it would only save them $4.75 million in cap space so it’s certainly not something they have to do.

item.title: Packers agreed to terms with WR Skyy Moore, formerly of the 49ers, on a one-year contract.
item.description: Moore primarily operated as a return man for the 49ers last season, returning 25 punts for 291 yards and 33 kicks for 907 yards. This included a 98-yard kick return, though he did not score on the play. Moore adds little as a receiver, tallying just five receptions for 87 yards last season after seeing just three targets in 2024 with the Chiefs. He will likely be used as a return specialist by the Packers.

item.title: Giants agree to terms with K Jason Sanders, formerly of the Dolphins, on a one-year contract.
item.description: The financial terms were not immediately made available. Sanders kicked for the Dolphins for seven years before being unable to kick in 2025 due to a hip injury. A career 84.6% field goal conversion rate is tied for 27th all time in the NFL, Sanders has the chance to stick with the Giants on a long term deal should he find similar successes in 2026.

item.title: Saints traded P Kai Kroeger to the Texans for a pick swap.
item.description: The two teams swap sixth and seventh-round picks in 2028 in a deal that sends Kroeger to Houston. This also likely means the team is moving on from veteran punter Tommy Townsend, who averaged just 41.6 net yards per punt (18th) last season in the final year of his two-year deal.

item.title: Saints agreed to terms with LB Kaden Elliss, formerly of the Falcons, on a three-year, $33 million contract.
item.description: Elliss gets $23 million guaranteed in a contract carrying a max value up to $39 million. He returns to New Orleans, where he spent the first four years of his career as a backup. Spending the last three seasons as a starter for the Falcons, the 30-year-old tallied 3.5 sacks in 2025 with an 83.6 PFF pass rush grade, No. 4 among linebackers. Elliss will slot in as a starter with Demario Davis leaving the Saints this offseason.

item.title: Eagles agreed to terms with CB Riq Woolen, formerly of the Seahawks, on a one-year, $15 million contract.
item.description: The Giants were reportedly the frontrunners to land Woolen’s services in free agency, with Howie Roseman scooping the veteran cornerback off the market for what appears to be a team-friendly deal. Woolen had been a consistent performer for the Seahawks during his four years with the franchise, although it is fair to say he never fully broke out as the team had planned. Perhaps there is further upside to be added to his resume under the aggression of Philadelphia defensive coordinator Vic Fangio.

item.title: Bears agreed to terms with DL Kentavius Street, formerly of the Falcons.
item.description: Street has spent the past three seasons as a rotational pass rusher for the Falcons. He notched three tackles for loss, including two sacks, in seven games last season. The Bears add Street for depth on the defensive line.

item.title: Commanders agreed to terms with LB Leo Chenal, formerly of the Chiefs, on a three-year, $24.75 million contract.
item.description: The veteran linebacker notched 44 starts across 65 appearances for the Chiefs while on his rookie contract, a stretch that also included two Super Bowl wins. Chenal averaged 57 tackles and 1.8 sacks per season those four years and earns a significant raise with his new franchise. It is unclear where he is likeliest to play considering the team already has Frankie Luvu and Bobby Wagner, but the kind of money invested hints at significant playing time.

item.title: 49ers re-signed LB Garret Wallow to a one-year contract.
item.description: Wallow was solid defensive depth for the 49ers, starting in two playoff games this past season. He is also a career special-teamer and will likely spend most of his time on special teams with San Francisco, barring linebacker injuries that thrust him back into a starting role.

item.title: Lions re-signed CB Rock Ya-Sin to a one-year, $4 million contract.
item.description: Ya-Sin first signed with the Lions last offseason and went on to play in 17 games, starting in six. He tallied nine passes defended and finished with a 64.7 PFF grade, good for No. 5 among cornerbacks. The Lions will bring him back as depth in the secondary.
